2603.22096.title: GSEM: Graph-based Self-Evolving Memory for Experience Augmented Clinical Reasoning
2603.22096.paper_summary: Clinical decision-making agents can benefit from reusing prior decision experience. However, many memory-augmented methods store experiences as independent records without explicit relational structure, which may introduce noisy retrieval, unreliable reuse, and in some cases even hurt performance compared to direct LLM inference. We propose GSEM (Graph-based Self-Evolving Memory), a clinical memory framework that organizes clinical experiences into a dual-layer memory graph, capturing both the decision structure within each experience and the relational dependencies across experiences, and supporting applicability-aware retrieval and online feedback-driven calibration of node quality and edge weights. Across MedR-Bench and MedAgentsBench with two LLM backbones, GSEM achieves the highest average accuracy among all baselines, reaching 70.90\% and 69.24\% with DeepSeek-V3.2 and Qwen3.5-35B, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/xhan1022/gsem.
2603.22096.paper_summary_zh: 臨床決策代理可以從重複使用先前的決策經驗中受益。<lb/>然而，許多記憶增強方法將經驗存儲為獨立的記錄，沒有明確的關聯結構，這可能會導致噪音檢索、不可靠的重用，並且在某些情況下，甚至會影響性能，與直接的LLM推斷相比。<lb/>我們提出了GSEM（基於圖的自我演化記憶），這是一個臨床記憶框架，將臨床經驗組織成雙層記憶圖，捕捉每個經驗中的決策結構以及經驗之間的關聯依賴，並支持應用感知的檢索和基於在線反饋的節點質量和邊權重的校準。<lb/>在MedR-Bench和MedAgentsBench上，使用兩個LLM骨幹，GSEM在所有基準中達到了最高的平均準確率，分別達到70.90\%和69.24\%，使用DeepSeek-V3.2和Qwen3.5-35B。<lb/>代碼可在https://github.com/xhan1022/gsem獲得。
2603.22096.author: Xiao Han et.al.
2603.22096.authors: Xiao Han, Yuzheng Fan, Sendong Zhao, Haochun Wang, Bing Qin
